name: KrillApp.DataPoint.Filter.Debounce
title: Debounce
description: Rejects DataPoint snapshots that arrive too quickly after the previous one. Compares the time delta between the new and previous snapshot timestamps against a configured minimum interval in milliseconds, stored in this filter's own meta.snapshot.value as a stringified number. Discards the snapshot if it arrives before the interval has elapsed. Rate-limits data updates to reduce storage and processing load. The filter only takes effect when the owning DataPoint lists it in its meta.inputs — parenting alone does nothing.
shortDescription: Discards snapshots that arrive faster than a configured minimum time interval.
llmPurpose: Rate-limits incoming DataPoint snapshots by rejecting updates that arrive before a configured minimum time interval has elapsed since the previous snapshot.
llmBehavior: compares timestamp delta against the minimum interval in its own meta.snapshot.value
discards snapshot if time since previous is too short
llmInputs: minimum interval in milliseconds stored in this filter's meta.snapshot.value (stringified number)
llmTypicalUseCases: reduce data volume from fast sensors
llmPromptHints: only accept one reading per second
llmCreationHints: Create a Debounce filter when the user wants to limit how frequently data is recorded. Store the interval in milliseconds (e.g., 1000 for one reading per second) as a stringified number in the filter's meta.snapshot.value (FilterMetaData has no value field), and list the filter in the owning DataPoint's meta.inputs — a filter merely parented under a DataPoint does nothing.

llmConnectionHints.role: leaf filter node that gates incoming snapshot values based on timing
llmConnectionHints.wiring: Passive: this filter uses no sources or invocationTriggers; the owning DataPoint must list it in its meta.inputs for it to be evaluated during ingest, and the interval lives in the filter's own meta.snapshot.value.

llmExamples: Only accept one reading per second (1000ms debounce).
Limit this sensor to one update every 5 seconds.
Debounce this switch to prevent rapid toggling.
